title: Once a piped dream, now a necessity
description: Disruptions triggered by the West Asia war have upended LPG supply chains, pushing households, hotels and industries alike to turn to piped natural gas (PNG) across Telangana. Gas distribution companies are speeding up network expansion, though adoption remains largely urban and cluster-driven. While PNG offers uninterrupted supply and more convenience, challenges such as infrastructure costs and last-mile connectivity persist, even as the State positions it as a more reliable, cleaner alternative to conventional cooking fuel, writes N. Ravi Kumar

Improving cleft care in the age of AI
description: In India, an estimated 30,000 children are born each year with cleft lip and/or palate. While surgical access has improved significantly, the gap lies beyond the operating room. In this context, AI is both a clinical tool and a bridge connecting various domains of comprehensive cleft care

title: Strengthening sustainable eye care infrastructure in India
description: While traditional models often focus on high-volume services in urban centres, sustainable models place greater emphasis on early intervention at the community level. In India, what is needed is centres that are  low-maintenance, community-based and resilient to local conditions

title: Annatto seed growers pitch for government support
description: Cultivated on nearly 10,000 acres, these seeds are the world’s second most important natural colourant after caramel. Thanks to impressive laboratory tests confirming seed quality and organic farming practices, they are in high demand across the European Union and the Middle East. However, the absence of government-backed organic certification, a proper procurement mechanism, and adequate storage facilities is forcing farmers to sell their produce at throwaway prices to middlemen, writes T. Appalanaidu.

title: Inside Andhra’s millet push that is turning women into entrepreneurs
description: Across Andhra Pradesh, a government initiative is building women-led millet enterprises that link farms, kitchens and local markets into viable livelihoods

title: Oil prices today: Crude falls in early trade on hopes of easing Middle East conflict
description: Oil prices dipped as hopes for a Middle East peace deal surged following a ceasefire and potential US-Iran talks. While a temporary agreement might be close, concerns over significant supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure persist. Experts anticipate continued price volatility until lasting peace and open trade routes are restored.

title: 'Targeting commercial shipping unacceptable': India urges safe navigation through Hormuz at UN
description: India has voiced strong concerns at the United Nations regarding threats to commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Ambassador Harish P emphasized the vital importance of maritime security for India's energy and economic interests. He condemned attacks on civilian maritime activity and called for the immediate restoration of safe navigation.

title: Nitish Kumar Reddy Interview: ‘I have overdone things… now I realise that’
description: In an exclusive conversation with TimesofIndia.com, the all-rounder opens up on the need of not overdoing things, the mental toughness, awareness about his game and the dream of playing for India in 2027 ODI World Cup.

title: 'Broke down in tears': Artemis II crew recall 'otherworldly' experience of their trip to the Moon
description: Artemis II astronauts shared their historic Moon journey's impact, fostering global unity and profound personal bonds.  Commander Wiseman highlighted the crew's unbreakable connection, while mission specialist Koch was moved by the worldwide pride.  The crew also recounted the intense re-entry and the awe-inspiring view of Earth from deep space, emphasizing humanity's collective achievement.

title: Just weeks before big Siri launch, Apple may send dozens of engineers to bootcamp
description: Apple is sending Siri engineers to an AI coding bootcamp just weeks before WWDC 2026, where a revamped voice assistant is expected. This move highlights the Siri team's lag in adopting AI-assisted tools, unlike other Apple divisions.  The company's struggles with Siri's reliability and a significant deal with Google for AI models underscore the urgency to fix its voice assistant.

Aryna Sabalenka vs Coco Gauff: Who is the richer tennis icon
description: Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff are two of the biggest stars in women’s tennis today. Both icons have won Grand Slam titles. They have ruled on the top of the WTA rankings. There is no doubt that they are among the highest earners in the sport. While Sabalenka dominates with power and consistency, Gauff shines with speed and confidence.

title: Lightning-Canadiens playoff opener delayed over arena and broadcast conflicts
description: Playoff opener between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa bay lightening has been postponed because of issues with arena availability and the broadcast schedule. The shift moves Game 1 to Sunday to give both teams more time to prepare. Having rested key players and having all logistics done, the next question is how the re-worked schedule would affect the early momentum in this much-hyped NHL game.

title: Dianna Russini’s divorce remarks resurface amid Mike Vrabel affair rumors
description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini, the popular NFL insider, have millions fuming ever since their pictures from a luxurious hotel in Arizona went viral, a few days ago. While both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied affair rumors, the allegations refuse to die down. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini has spoken about some issues in her marriage.
